case_name: Tammie Gardner v. William Dotie Jackson, M.D., and Mississippi Premier Plastic Surgery PLLC

summary.brief: Tammie Gardner v. William Dotie Jackson, M.D., and Mississippi Premier Plastic Surgery PLLC. Medical malpractice case involving other.

evidence_index.text: On March 12, 2014, Dr. William Dotie Jackson performed a breast augmentation and mastopexy operation on Tammie Gardner.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: After the surgery, both of Gardner’s breasts became infected and ultimately had to be removed.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On March 17, 2014, Gardner returned to Dr. Jackson’s office for her first follow-up appointment.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Gardner told Dr. Jackson that she had a low-grade fever the night before, but after she took Tylenol, her fever subsided.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On March 24, 2014, Gardner returned for her next follow-up visit.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Gardner told Dr. Jackson that she had a “blood blister” next to her right nipple.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On March 28, 2014, Gardner left a voicemail at Dr. Jackson’s office, complaining of a fever and “pus-like” drainage from her left nipple.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On that Monday, Gardner arrived at Dr. Jackson’s office. She was “very ill.”
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Jackson rushed Gardner to surgery, where he operated on her left breast in an attempt to save her breast from requiring removal.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Jackson, nevertheless, had to remove Gardner’s left breast in a subsequent operation.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: After the removal of the left breast, Gardner’s right breast started to show signs of infection, and after an additional surgery, Dr. Jackson removed Gardner’s right breast as well.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Nease testified that “[t]he decision to make about which pedicle [a surgeon is] going to utilize in a patient like Mrs. Gardner . . . [is] critically important” to preserve blood flow to the breast tissue.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Nease

evidence_index.text: Dr. Nease explained that “pedicle planning is critically important,” and not compromising blood flow to the breast is “one of the most basic things you teach.”
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Nease

evidence_index.text: The trial court granted the motion for a directed verdict in favor of both defendants, finding that Gardner’s expert never established the nationally recognized standard of care.
evidence_index.speaker: Trial court

evidence_index.text: Gardner failed to present expert testimony that established an objective, nationally accepted standard of care, she failed to make her prima facie case.
evidence_index.speaker: Court reasoning

evidence_index.text: For the reasons stated above, we find that the trial court did not err in granting Dr. Jackson and Mississippi Premier Plastic Surgery PLLC’s motion for a directed verdict.
evidence_index.speaker: Court conclusion

initial_state.chief_complaint: Pain in both breasts, asymmetry, and hardening of implants
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient presented with complaints of pain in both breasts, asymmetry, and hardening of implants. Previous surgeries included breast reduction and augmentation with no complications.

focused_exam.breasts: Swollen breasts, intact incisions, no drainage initially

critical_findings: Blood blister next to right nipple

reveal.result_summary: Patient reported low-grade fever, blood blister, and later pus-like drainage
reveal.decision_impact: Should prompt further investigation and possible intervention

timeline_phases.name: Initial Consultation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents with breast pain and implant issues

decision_points.clinical_context: Pre-operative planning for breast augmentation and mastopexy
decision_points.prompt: What pedicle approach should be used for the surgery?

options.clinical_reasoning: Maintains blood supply consistent with previous surgeries
Contradicts previous surgical approach, risking blood supply

actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Defendant initially planned inferior pedicle but did not update plan after reviewing previous surgery notes

expected_action_court.description: Superior pedicle should have been used
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony indicated importance of maintaining consistent pedicle approach

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to adjust surgical plan based on previous surgeries
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Superior pedicle should have been used to maintain blood supply
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: Both breasts became infected and were removed
patient_outcome.long_term_effects: Loss of both breasts, potential psychological impact

key_findings: Plaintiff failed to establish the nationally accepted standard of care
Directed verdict granted in favor of defendants

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to establish standard of care
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Not applicable as no breach was legally established

treatments.outcome: Attempt to save breast, ultimately removed

complications.complication: Infection leading to removal of both breasts

allegations.allegation: Failure to use correct pedicle approach

court_findings.finding: No breach of standard of care established
court_findings.legal_significance: Directed verdict in favor of defendants
